title: koeleria cristata subsp. transsilvanica
intro: Koeleria cristata subsp. transsilvanica, also known as the Transylvanian Koeleria or the mountain hair grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-bacterial, and anti-viral activities. Koeleria cristata subsp. transsilvanica is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to promote healing and well-being.
